title: Automation/IaC Foundation Lab Submission
description: Evidence template for introductory automation and Infrastructure-as-Code practice in ARIA.

required_evidence.label: Automation goal
required_evidence.description: Explain what task the automation or IaC workflow is supposed to perform.

required_evidence.label: Files or commands used
required_evidence.description: List the script, playbook, config, or commands used. Do not include secrets, tokens, vault passwords, or private keys.

required_evidence.label: Dry run or safety check
required_evidence.description: Show how you checked the automation before trusting it, such as syntax check, dry run, limited scope, or read-only test.

required_evidence.label: Execution result
required_evidence.description: Provide output or observations showing what happened when the automation ran.

required_evidence.label: Validation
required_evidence.description: Show how you proved the result was correct.

required_evidence.label: Rollback or recovery note
required_evidence.description: Explain how you would reverse, recover, or safely stop the change if something went wrong.

required_evidence.label: Professional summary
required_evidence.description: Write a short sysadmin-style summary of the automation task and result.

completion_signals: student identifies the files or commands used
student provides a safety check before execution
student includes rollback or recovery thinking
safety_rules: Do not submit passwords, API keys, SSH private keys, vault passwords, session cookies, or tokens.
Do not run destructive automation unless the lab explicitly instructs it.
Prefer dry-run, check mode, or read-only validation before changes.
Use limited scope before broad execution.
Explain rollback or recovery before marking the lab complete.
